content.problemStatement:
For a given array AAA of length NNN, we define f(A)f(A)f(A) in the following way:


Consider a directed graph on NNN vertices labelled 111, 222, ‚Ä¶\ldots‚Ä¶, NNN.
There exist a directed edge i‚Üíji \rightarrow ji‚Üíj if and only if i<j‚Č§i+Aii < j \le i + A_ii<j‚Č§i+Ai‚Äč.
f(A)f(A)f(A) is defined as the smallest number of edges on a directed path from vertex 111 to vertex NNN.
You are given integers NNN and PPP, and your task is to find the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all NNN^NNN possible arrays AAA of length NNN satisfying 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§N1 \le A_i \le N1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§N.

Since the answer may be large, you have to print it modulo PPP. It is guaranteed that PPP is prime.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and PPP.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the sum of f(A)f(A)f(A) over all NNN^NNN arrays AAA modulo PPP.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 600600600.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : There are 444 possible arrays [1,1][1, 1][1,1], [1,2][1, 2][1,2], [2,1][2, 1][2,1] and [2,2][2, 2][2,2]. All of them produce the same graph, i.e. there is only one edge 1‚Üí21 \rightarrow 21‚Üí2, and this graph has a path of length 111 from vertex 111 to vertex 222. Hence, the answer is 4‚čÖ1=44 \cdot 1 = 44‚čÖ1=4.
